profile.name: Surakiat Sangkla
profile.aboutMe: I’m a seasoned software engineer with over five years of expertise in system design and development. I currently serve as a Senior Engineer building backend services for a Virtual Bank project at Ascend Group, developing core payment APIs including Request to Pay and BAHTNET using Kotlin Spring Boot. I also champion AI-assisted development workflows and designing systems with GitHub Copilot CLI and GraphRAG to accelerate team productivity.

experience.description: I am part of a development team building backend services for a Virtual Bank project. As a Senior Engineer, I use Kotlin with Spring Boot to develop and maintain backend APIs supporting core payment systems including Request to Pay, BAHTNET, and Backend for Frontend (BFF). The system is deployed on AWS EC2 with PostgreSQL (Amazon Aurora) and Redis for caching. I also initiated AI-assisted development using GitHub Copilot CLI with GraphRAG and skills.md to read Jira Cards, PRDs, and Confluence Specs, generating code that matches team standards over 90% and reducing tasks from 2 sprints to just 3 days.

I was part of a development team working on a new internal system project for TTB as a contract-based role. I used Java Spring Boot to develop new backend APIs replacing legacy systems, focusing on core banking functions including fund transfer, deposit, withdrawal, and payment systems. Developed and implemented new RESTful APIs improving system stability and reducing response time by 40%. Built comprehensive error handling and logging systems, reducing debugging time by 50%.

I was a member of the team providing foreign exchange services, managing products such as Spot, Forward, and FX Swap for international payments in major currencies. I used Java Spring Boot for backend development, PostgreSQL and Redis for data caching, and Azure with Jenkins for infrastructure management. Developed project templates and structures resulting in 60% faster project initiation. Created a data migration system for large CSV and Excel files, leading to a 50% increase in data upload speed. Developed a currency exchange system for Thai Baht and foreign currencies for use in department stores and airports.

I worked in the Development department using JavaScript (VueJS, NuxtJS) and HTML5, Bootstrap for frontend management, and Lua for backend management. I managed and developed products related to corporate data storage and analysis using PostgreSQL, MongoDB, and Redis. Implemented a distributed caching system improving system performance by 70%. Developed and managed a monitoring and notification system, reducing system downtime by 90% and incident response time by 80%.

volunteer.description: Greenpeace is an independent global campaigning network that acts to change attitudes and behaviour, to protect and conserve the environment and to promote peace. It comprises 26 independent national/regional Greenpeace organisations with presence in over 55 countries across Europe, Africa, the Americas, Asia and the Pacific, as well as a coordinating and supporting organisation, Greenpeace International.

project.name: TypeScript
project.description: TypeScript is a language for application-scale JavaScript. TypeScript adds optional types to JavaScript that support tools for large-scale JavaScript applications for any browser, for any host, on any OS. TypeScript compiles to readable, standards-based JavaScript.
